segments.text: Keep my words, and lay up my commandments with thee.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Father

segments.text: Keep my commandments, and live; and my law as the apple of thine eye.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Father

segments.text: Bind them upon thy fingers, write them upon the table of thine heart.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Father

segments.text: Say unto wisdom, Thou art my sister; and call understanding thy kinswoman:
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Father

segments.text: That they may keep thee from the strange woman, from the stranger which flattereth with her words.
For at the window of my house I looked through my casement,
And beheld among the simple ones, I discerned among the youths, a young man void of understanding,
Passing through the street near her corner; and he went the way to her house,
In the twilight, in the evening, in the black and dark night:

footnotes: in the evening: Heb. in the evening of days

segments.text: And, behold, there met him a woman with the attire of an harlot, and subtil of heart.
(She is loud and stubborn; her feet abide not in her house:
Now is she without, now in the streets, and lieth in wait at every corner.)
So she caught him, and kissed him,
with an impudent face said unto him,
segments.speaker: Adulteress

footnotes: she strengthened her face and said

segments.text: I have peace offerings with me; this day have I payed my vows.
segments.speaker: Adulteress

segments.text: Therefore came I forth to meet thee, diligently to seek thy face, and I have found thee.
segments.speaker: Adulteress

segments.text: I have decked my bed with coverings of tapestry, with carved works, with fine linen of Egypt.
segments.speaker: Adulteress

segments.text: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
segments.speaker: Adulteress

segments.text: Come, let us take our fill of love until the morning: let us solace ourselves with loves.
segments.speaker: Adulteress

segments.text: For the goodman is not at home, he is gone a long journey:
segments.speaker: Adulteress

segments.text: He hath taken a bag of money with him, and will come home at the day appointed.
segments.speaker: Adulteress

footnotes: with him: Heb. in his hand
the day appointed: or, the new moon

segments.text: With her much fair speech she caused him to yield, with the flattering of her lips she forced him.
He goeth after her straightway, as an ox goeth to the slaughter, or as a fool to the correction of the stocks;
Till a dart strike through his liver; as a bird hasteth to the snare, and knoweth not that it is for his life.
Hearken unto me now therefore, O ye children, and attend to the words of my mouth.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Father

segments.text: Let not thine heart decline to her ways, go not astray in her paths.
segments.speaker: Wisdom/Father

segments.text: For she hath cast down many wounded: yea, many strong men have been slain by her.
Her house is the way to hell, going down to the chambers of death.
